conceptualization.description: Focus on the intricate details of various flowers

technique.color_strategy: Use of light, fluid watercolors to allow spontaneous color blending and bleeding. The overarching palette should be soft yet diverse, including pastel shades that evoke the freshness and delicacy of a garden.
technique.details_focus: Each flower, though abstract, will be defined by variations in tone and texture rather than clear lines. Techniques like wet-on-wet, dry brush, and salt texturing can be employed to mimic the fragile details of petals and pollen.

composition.layout: The composition should be dynamic and appear organic, as if strolling through a garden. Flowers should be at various stages of bloom, from bud to full bloom, arranged so that detailed areas flow into less defined washes of color, mimicking the way flowers might be clustered in a garden.

interpretive_elements.emotional_tone: The painting aims to evoke a serene, contemplative mood, encouraging the viewer to delve into the blend of colors and perhaps discover hidden forms and details upon closer inspection.
interpretive_elements.viewer_interaction: As a reverse color page, the artwork should allow for personal interpretation and interaction, inviting viewers to imagine their own garden through the layers of colors and forms.
